If you want the best experience, upgrading to Premium is a must—but between the massive catalog and offline downloading, it is worth every penny.

title: Free tier on iPhone is deliberately worse and Spotify admits it
text:
Spotify’s free experience is NOT equal across devices, and that’s unacceptable. On Windows, macOS, and even iPadOS, free users get full on-demand listening. On iPhone and Android? You get a daily on-demand cap — once you hit it, Spotify forces you into shuffle mode, even mid-playlist. Spotify’s own community moderators openly confirmed: “The iPad has some advantages over the mobile version, just like the desktop apps.”

Mobile free users are also stuck at 160kbps audio quality while desktop free users get better. This isn’t a technical limitation — it’s a deliberate design choice to pressure mobile users into paying. If you’re happy to listen free on your Mac or iPad, great. The moment you open the iPhone app, you’re a second-class user.

One star until the free tier is consistent across all platforms. The double standard is insulting.
core_frustration: Smart shuffle repeating same tracks or not feeling truly random.

title: You are ruining this app with AI
text: Have been premium for years. Never a single problem except for in the past year the app has gotten significantly noticeably worse, to the point that using it is infuriating . Incredibly slow and glitchy, no matter how much I clear my cache. No, I am not going to accept that ‘having too many liked songs’ is a factor as these changes have only happened in the past year or so. STOP letting AI write your code. Stop adding TikTok features - it’s a music app not a scrolling app. It was completely fine the way it used to be. I know that I am not the only user experience this slowness. Jamming it full of useless AI features that no one needs is just ruining the app experience. I have been an avid Spotify premium user for years but I am now considering switching my family to a different platform because the app is now barely functional. I doubt this review will have any effect as developers are obviously more concerned with AI-ing the app as much as possible for some sick reason instead of maintaining the reliable usable app they had before. title: Dismal search, awful information
text:
Search has always been bad - but as the offering increases, you’re often stuck with the first tranche of results with no concept of page two. Adding information to the search usually just adds bad results rather reordering good ones to the top.

There’s no real way see information from a long title. Anything too far left is forever invisible. Tables visible on the desktop app aren’t to be found on the mobile app! Why? Then again in the absence of columns sorting and filtering- tables on the desktop are no good if it doesn’t fit on a single screen.

I can’t define a subsection of a long musical piece in a playlist. In opera that often means you’re stuck with 10 minutes surrounding the 3 minute aria or chorus you want.

These all feel one intern week away from implementing. Boo!


In some places locally searching a playlist now works. Good.

title: Why can’t Spotify also support small artists?
text: Unfortunately many musicians and smaller artists have grievances that include the dismal royalty payouts, the 1,000-stream minimum threshold required to generate revenue, and a pro-rata model that disproportionately benefits megastars. Spotify instituted a policy where tracks failing to hit at least 1,000 streams in a 12-month period no longer generate royalties. Critics and industry advocates argue this demonetizes approximately 87% of all tracks on the platform, disproportionately hurting smaller, niche, and independent artists.

title: Smart Shuffle is a joke, it plays the same 10 songs on repeat
text: Is anyone else getting extremely frustrated with Spotify's Smart Shuffle? I have a playlist with over 800 songs. Every time I turn on Smart Shuffle, it injects 'recommended' tracks. But it's literally the same 10-15 songs every single day! I keep hitting skip, but Spotify refuses to take the hint. It feels like they are pushing sponsored tracks or songs that are cheap for them to stream. The algorithm is completely broken and repetitive.
core_frustration: Smart shuffle repeating same tracks or not feeling truly random.

title: One focus session ruined my entire Discover Weekly
text: I listened to a 4-hour 'Deep Focus Lofi' playlist last week while studying for an exam. Now, my Discover Weekly is 90% lofi beats. I don't want to listen to lofi in my normal life! Why does Spotify's algorithm weight a single focus session so heavily? It has completely contaminated my music profile. There should be a private listening session that doesn't affect my recommendations, or a way to manually remove genres from my taste profile.
core_frustration: Single listening session (focus/kids) permanently poisoning recommendations.

title: Spotify DJ is just a repeater
text: The AI DJ started off cool, but now it's just repeating itself. Every single day it goes: 'Let's take a look back at what you were listening to in 2022' or 'Here is your daily dose of your favorite genre, Indie Pop.' It rarely introduces me to new artists. When it does try to play 'something new,' it's always high-profile pop songs that I already know. Where is the actual discovery? I feel like I'm stuck in a feedback loop.
core_frustration: Recommendation engine falling back to familiar tracks or creating an echo chamber.

title: Why does Spotify radio just play my liked songs?
text: If I start a 'Song Radio' based on an obscure synthwave track, the first 5 songs are synthwave, but then the next 15 are songs from my 'Liked Songs' that have absolutely nothing to do with the original vibe. It's like the algorithm gets lazy and falls back to what it knows I like. I start radio to find *new* music, not to listen to the same Tame Impala songs for the thousandth time. This is lazy engineering.
core_frustration: Recommendation engine falling back to familiar tracks or creating an echo chamber.

title: No way to discover music by complex moods
text: I want to find music that feels like 'driving in the rain in a cyberpunk city' or 'nostalgic autumn afternoon'. If I search these terms, I just get user-created playlists with questionable curation, or generic mood hubs from Spotify (e.g. 'Chill', 'Sad'). The recommendation engine has no understanding of context, mood, or lyrics. It only matches acoustic features and user co-occurrence. We need an AI that actually understands descriptions.
core_frustration: Recommendation engine falling back to familiar tracks or creating an echo chamber.

title: Comment on: Recommendations are getting worse?
text: Totally agree. I feel like Spotify is actively discouraging discovery to save on royalty costs. Playing independent long-tail music might cost them more or they want to push major label content. I keep getting recommended the same mainstream tracks even though I listen to very niche subgenres. Collaborative filtering just creates a massive echo chamber where everyone is pushed to the center.
core_frustration: Recommendation engine falling back to familiar tracks or creating an echo chamber.

title: We need a 'Don't Recommend This Artist' button
text: Why is there no block button for artists or genres? There are certain artists I absolutely detest, yet Spotify keeps inserting them into my Release Radar and Discover Weekly. If I skip a song within 2 seconds, that should be a strong signal to never play it again. Instead, it plays it again tomorrow on a different playlist. We need direct controls over our recommendation profile, not just a passive thumbs up/down.
core_frustration: Recommendation engine falling back to familiar tracks or creating an echo chamber.

title: Discovery is too much work
text: As I've gotten older, I don't have hours to spend digging through music blogs or clicking through obscure albums. I want Spotify to do the heavy lifting, but their automated playlists are boring. Daily Mixes are just songs I already know, and Discover Weekly is a hit-or-miss. It feels like there is no middle ground between active digging (which is exhausting) and passive listening (which is repetitive).
core_frustration: Recommendation engine falling back to familiar tracks or creating an echo chamber.

title: Comment on: Shuffle algorithm is broken
text: It definitely is not random. It is optimized to keep you on the app, which apparently means playing your top 5% most listened songs over and over. I have a 2000-song playlist and I swear I hear the same 100 songs. It's frustrating because I want to rediscover songs I saved 5 years ago, but the algorithm hides them in favor of my recent habits. I want a true random shuffle, or a shuffle that prioritizes 'least played' tracks.
core_frustration: Smart shuffle repeating same tracks or not feeling truly random.

title: Shared account or family plan recommendations are a mess
text: My kid uses my Spotify account in the car, and now my recommendations are a blend of heavy metal and Cocomelon. Spotify's recommendation engine has zero awareness of multi-user profiles or contextual situations (like car vs. gym vs. home). The AI should easily be able to tell that nursery rhymes are a separate context and shouldn't bleed into my metal discover playlist.
core_frustration: Recommendation engine falling back to familiar tracks or creating an echo chamber.
